hero.title: World's Greatest Dad
hero.reason: In honor of World Vitiligo Day, a film that tackles complex social issues including self-acceptance with a blend of humor and drama.
hero.blurb: A dark comedy that shines with its thought-provoking narrative and outstanding performances, making it a must-watch for those who enjoy unconventional storytelling. The movie's ability to balance humor and serious themes is commendable. With its unique approach, it stands out as a memorable film.

released.title: Evan Almighty
released.reason: Released today in 2007, this film still brings laughter and valuable life lessons with its entertaining storyline and impressive cast.
released.blurb: A funny and heartwarming movie that reminds us of the importance of family and following one's dreams. With its lighthearted tone and engaging plot, it's a great choice for a family movie night. The blend of comedy and drama makes it enjoyable for a wide range of audiences.

birthday.title: George of the Jungle
birthday.reason: Happy birthday to George of the Jungle star Brendan Fraser, born June 25, 1968, starring in this classic family comedy that showcases his acting talent and charisma.
birthday.blurb: A fun and adventurous film that brings a modern twist to the classic tale, with Brendan Fraser's charming performance as the lead. The movie's mix of humor, action, and heart makes it a delightful watch for both kids and adults.

awful.title: I Know Who Killed Me
awful.reason: A famously bad movie with terrible reviews, I Know Who Killed Me is a thriller that failed to impress audiences with its confusing plot and poor execution.
awful.blurb: This movie is a mess of cliches and unoriginal ideas, making it a prime example of how not to make a thriller. With its low ratings and negative reviews, it's a film that's better left unwatched unless you're looking for a laugh at its expense.
